rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. Defendant Campbell Soup Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is continued on the court¿s own motion to April 5. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CRONIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion Under C.C.P. § 473(B) To Vacate The Order Granting Defendant Ford Motor Company¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to vacate the order granting defendant Ford Motor Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted on the condition that plaintiffs do not oppose a request by defendant to continue the trial date to allow sufficient time for defendant¿s motion for summary judgment to be heard and, if denied, for defendant to prepare for trial. Plaintiffs¿ failure to speak with Mr. Aahl prior to the hearing date on the earlier motion is excusable neglect. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JEFF BRADSHAW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 3. Defendant Albay Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to include a response to a special interrogatory asking plaintiff for all facts supporting his contentions that defendant caused him to be exposed to asbestos, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ORMAN SMITH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 4. Defendant Moore Bros.¿ motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that plaintiff was exposed to respirable fibers from an asbestos-containing product supplied by defendant. Plaintiff¿s responses to contention discovery served by defendant state that plaintiff has knowledge that he was exposed to asbestos-containing welding materials, including asbestos gloves and welding rods, supplied by defendant. The excerpts of plaintiff¿s deposition testimony submitted with the moving papers do not show that plaintiff lacks such knowledge. Furthermore, at his deposition plaintiff testified that Andy Thompson and Tom Boyd might have knowledge about plaintiff¿s work ¿as it relates to Graybar or Moore Brothers¿ (1225: 2-8) yet defendant¿s separate statement contains no reference to either Mr. Thompson or Mr. Boyd, much less any evidence that these men lack knowledge that plaintiff was exposed to respirable fibers from an asbestos-containing product supplied by defendant. Defendant is admonished that reference to ¿Exhibits A through D¿, without any greater specificity, as supporting evidence in its separate statement (see purported undisputed material facts 9, 12 and 13) is insufficient and poses significant burdens on opposing counsel and the court in evaluating whether any purported undisputed fact is in fact undisputed. Even if defendant satisfied its burden of production, plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to respirable fibers from an asbestos-containing product supplied by defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KIM MORGAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 5. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is continued on the court¿s own motion to April 5. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KIM MORGAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 5. Defendant H&C Investment Associates, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. The motion for summary judgment is denied because defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either of the two prongs enunciated in Aguilar. By not presenting any substantive responses to contention discovery, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant and that any such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff¿s asbestos-related illness.. The declarations of Dr. Rabnovitz and Dr. Sandler do not negate an element of plaintiff¿s claims because those declarations are largely inadmissible, assume that the only percipient witness that plaintiff has to prove his claims is plaintiff himself (as to which there is no evidence), further assumes (contrary to plaintiff¿s deposition) that a Childers product never got on the hands of plaintiff, and still further assumes that such product was never in a dry state on plaintiff¿s hands (as to which there is no evidence) . Plaintiff¿s objections to the declarations of Dr. Rabinovitz and Dr. Sandler are sustained. The motion for summary adjudication of punitive damages is denied because defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Plaintiff¿s responses to contention discovery regarding punitive damages that only contain objections do not provide a basis to draw the necessary inference for the successful invocation of the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. (Gaggero v. Yura (2003) 108 Cal. App 4th 884, 892). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KIM MORGAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: ART HOOVER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 7. Defendant Performance Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed March 21. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ART HOOVER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 7. Defendant Industry Products Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant, that defendant made a false representation about the product, or that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. By failing to submit plaintiff¿s responses to specially-prepared contention discovery, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The mere fact, without more, that plaintiff testified at his deposition that he does not recall the name Industrial Products Company is insufficient to satisfy defendant¿s burden or production. = (220/HEK)
